nuggets.headline: Billie Eilish's debut album was recorded in her brother Finneas's bedroom
nuggets.text: The entirety of "WHEN WE ALL FALL ASLEEP, WHERE DO WE GO?" — including "bad guy" — was written and produced by Billie Eilish and her older brother, Finneas O'Connell, in his small bedroom studio in Highland Park, Los Angeles. Billie was only seventeen when the album was released, making the intimate, DIY recording process a cornerstone of their early success. This personal approach is fundamental to the album's distinct feel.

source.title: FEATURE: you should see me in a crown: Billie Eilish’s Phenomenal Debut Album, WHEN WE ALL FALL ASLEEP, WHERE DO WE GO?, at Five — Music Musings & Such
source.quoteSnippet: WHEN WE ALL FALL ASLEEP, WHERE DO WE GO? was written by Billie Eilish and Finneas O’Connell, who produced it at his small bedroom studio in Highland Park, Los Angeles.

nuggets.imageCaption: Billie Eilish's debut album cover captures her distinctive aesthetic.

nuggets.headline: Billie Eilish's brother Finneas sampled a street crossing signal for "bad guy"
nuggets.text: The distinct production of "bad guy" includes an unusual sonic element: a pedestrian street crossing signal. While on tour in Australia, Finneas O'Connell and Billie Eilish recorded the rhythmic sound made when the 'walk' sign turns on in Sydney and Melbourne, incorporating it into the song's melody. Finneas often seeks out unique, everyday sounds to add a textural quality to his productions, aiming to set his work apart from others.

source.title: Meaning Behind the Song: "Bad Guy" by Billie Eilish -
source.quoteSnippet: One example is when he and his sister were on tour in Australia, they noticed that the street crossing signal for pedestrians made a distinct sound that they recorded and incorporated into part of the melody of “Bad Guy.”

nuggets.headline: Lana Del Rey influenced Billie Eilish's early musical direction
nuggets.text: If you're looking to explore more artists who helped shape the sound of Billie Eilish, turn to Lana Del Rey. Eilish has cited Lana Del Rey as a clear influence on her own music, with early tracks like "Ocean Eyes" featuring a vocal style described as "Lana Del Rey-indebted crooning." Del Rey's ability to blend emotional depth with innovative production elements set a precedent that Eilish followed. Dive into Lana Del Rey's 2019 album, "Norman F***ing Rockwell!", for a masterclass in atmospheric pop.

source.title: Billie Eilish: When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go? Album Review | Pitchfork
source.quoteSnippet: At 14, she put the song “ Ocean Eyes” on SoundCloud, a glassy, straightforward ballad with tearful synths and woozy, Lana Del Rey–indebted crooning.

nuggets.imageCaption: Lana Del Rey, a noted influence on Billie Eilish's artistic journey.

artistSummary: Billie Eilish Pirate Baird O'Connell, known professionally as Billie Eilish, exploded onto the music scene as a teenager with her genre-defying sound. She quickly became a global icon, recognized for her haunting vocals, introspective lyrics, and unique artistic vision.
